preamble: Debuccalization of the voiceless aspirated velar stop [kʰ] to the glottal fricative [h] is a common end-stage in many lenition chains, notably in the development of the Germanic languages.

examples.note: Grimm's Law final stage: PIE *k > kʰ > x > h (e.g. PIE *kmtom > English hundred).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
